result:
**Goal: Add a Cardiac Electrophysiology & Arrhythmia simulation mode.**

Simulate the heart's electrical conduction system as a 2D tissue slab with: SA node pacemaker cells auto-firing at ~60 bpm, AV node delay gating, bundle of His / Purkinje fiber fast-conduction pathways, and action potential wavefronts propagating through atrial and ventricular myocyte grids (depolarization → plateau → repolarization → refractory period, modeled via simplified FitzHugh-Nagumo kinetics). Include ion channel dynamics (Na⁺ fast inward, Ca²⁺ plateau, K⁺ repolarization), re-entry circuit formation for arrhythmias, an ECG trace derived from summed dipole vectors across the tissue, and a defibrillation shock mechanic. Three views: tissue activation map with wavefront propagation and conduction anatomy, real-time 12-lead-style ECG strip, and time-series metrics. Presets: Normal Sinus Rhythm, Atrial Fibrillation (rapid chaotic atrial wavelets), Ventricular Tachycardia (re-entry spiral), AV Block (dropped beats), Long QT Syndrome (delayed repolarization → torsades), and Defibrillation Rescue.

This complements the existing angiogenesis/blood-vessel mode with the heart's *electrical* side, gives stunning spiral-wave terminal visuals, and is medically educational.
